instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
How SISO Helps You Benefit from Rupee Cost Averaging at Both Entry and Exit
Timing the market is of great significance when you are investing in market-linked avenues, like life insurance ULIPs. For generating good returns, it is important to know when to invest and when to withdraw. This is where Rupees Cost Averaging (RCA) comes to the rescue by spreading investments over time. Bajaj Allianz Life’s SISO (Systematic In Systematic Out) strategy further enhances this approach, as it works effectively during both accumulation and withdrawal phases. Read on to know in detail about RCA and how it is incorporated in the SISO system to reap maximum benefits.
What is Rupee Cost Averaging?
Rupee Cost Averaging is a clear investment technique, where you invest a fixed amount at regular intervals of time, regardless of how the market is performing. This disciplined approach helps to spread out the risk as you can buy more units when the prices are low, and fewer units when prices go up. With time, it helps to maintain an average NAV, thus reducing the impact of market ups and downs.
With SISO, you can enjoy the benefits of RCA both at the time of investing in a ULIP as well as when redeeming your fund value. This helps to handle market volatility and averages out investment risks .
How Does SISO Enhance RCA?
As explained above, SISO works at both ends – purchase and redemption, boosting RCA. Here’s how:
1. At Entry (Systematic In):
When you start investing in ULIPs via the SISO strategy, you choose to pay the premiums monthly. So, even if the market drops, after your initial investment, the purchase price can be averaged with systematic investment. Let us understand it clearly with an example:
Suppose you invest ₹5,000 in a ULIP every month.
In 3 months, you’ve invested ₹15,000 to purchase 152.57 units of the fund at an average rate of about ₹98.33 per unit, which is lower than the initial value of ₹100. If you had invested ₹15,000 in a lump sum at the start, when the NAV was ₹100, you would have got only 150 units. Thus, with SISO, you ended up having a higher number of units.
2. At Exit (Systematic Out)
Just like the buying rate, SISO assists you in averaging the redemption costs, too. Instead of taking out the complete amount in one go, you receive a fixed income at regular intervals. This prevents you from impulsively selling out all your funds. With disciplined withdrawal, you not only receive a continuous source of money, but the remaining funds stay invested in the market for a longer tenure. If the markets are rallying, staying invested can generate attractive returns.
Why is RCA Important in Volatile Markets?
It is difficult to predict market fluctuations with the NAV rising or going down all of a sudden. A lump sum investment or withdrawal during such periods may lead to huge losses. By using a disciplined approach of investment over time, Rupee Cost Averaging lowers the risk and smoothens the returns.
Benefits of RCA with SISO
Here’s how implementing RCA with SISO can help to get maximum benefits from your ULIPs:
Reduced Impact of Volatility
With the systematic approach to making payments and withdrawals, SISO protects you from sudden market fluctuations.
Disciplined Investing
Regular and systematic investments can help build a steady corpus over time without a considerable pocket pinch.
Better Risk-Adjusted Returns
The market highs and lows get balanced out with RCA, and you can enjoy steady returns on your investments.
Cash Flow Management
Systematic payouts ensure that you receive a fixed income at regular intervals, thereby managing your cash flow requirements.
Emotional Advantage
With SISO, you do not have to be under the pressure of “timing the market” correctly, as the systematic approach will smooth out the risks.
For Example/Scenario
Suppose you contribute ₹10,000 in ULIP every month for 10 years. Total premium amount paid will be ₹12,00,000. As the contributions are spread out evenly, you benefit via RCA. When the market goes up, you get fewer units, and when the NAV goes down, you receive higher units. As time passes, this balances the overall purchase costs and protects you from market swings.
After 10 years, you can withdraw the maturity benefit in the form of fixed monthly payouts under SISO. You can receive a fixed income every month, while the rest of the fund stays invested to yield additional returns.
SISO keeps you disciplined and free from market pressure to enjoy steady investments and withdrawals.
Who Should Use SISO for RCA Benefits?
SISO is helpful in the following cases:
For novice investors who are not experienced in handling market volatility.
People who are planning for retirement, children’s education, or other major milestones of life can take advantage of SISO by disciplined investment and systematic withdrawals.
Individuals looking for an alternative source of income can use SISO for a predictable monthly payout, while keeping the rest of the fund to grow.
Conclusion
Rupee Cost Averaging works well for investors who want steady growth without having to time the market. SISO provides the RCA benefit both when you buy the ULIP as well as on redemptions. With Bajaj Allianz life insurance plans, you can choose SISO and tackle market fluctuations through rupee-cost averaging. The disciplined approach to investment and redemption not only manages volatility but can also let you enjoy consistent returns to fulfil long-term goals.
Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER.
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789

How Bajaj Life Insurance SISO Strategy Helps You Benefit from Rupee Cost Averaging at Both Entry and Exit
Timing the market is of great significance when you are investing in market-linked avenues. For generating good returns, it is important to know when to invest and when to withdraw. This is where Rupees Cost Averaging (RCA) comes to the rescue by spreading investments over time. Bajaj Life Insurances SISO (Systematic in Systematic Out) strategy further enhances this approach, as it works effectively during both accumulation and withdrawal phases. Read on to know in detail about RCA and how it is incorporated in the SISO system to reap benefits.
What is Rupee Cost Averaging?
Rupee Cost Averaging is a clear investment technique, where you invest a fixed amount at regular intervals of time, regardless of how the market is performing. This disciplined approach helps to spread out the risk as you can buy more units when the prices are low, and fewer units when prices go up. With time, it helps to maintain an average NAV, thus reducing the impact of market ups and downs.
With SISO, you can enjoy the benefits of RCA both at the time of investing in a ULIP as well as when redeeming your fund value. This helps to handle market volatility and averages investment risks.
How Does SISO Enhance RCA?
As explained above, SISO works at both ends – purchase and redemption, boosting RCA. Here’s how:
1. At Entry (Systematic In):
When you start investing in ULIPs via the SISO strategy, you choose to pay the premiums monthly. It allows you to average the purchase price through systematic investments, even during market fluctuations, subject to applicable terms and conditions. So, even if the market drops, after your initial investment, the purchase price can be averaged with systematic investment. Let us understand it clearly with an example:
Note: It is important to note that the SISO strategy is a Bajaj Life-specific strategy which is applicable on select ULIPs only.
Suppose you invest ₹5,000 in a ULIP every month.
Month Premium (₹) Net Asset Value (₹) No. of Units Bought (Premium / NAV) 1 5,000 100 50 2 5,000 98 51.02 3 5,000 97 51.55 In 3 months, you’ve invested ₹15,000 to purchase 152.57 units of the fund at an average rate of about ₹98.33 per unit, which is lower than the initial value of ₹100. If you had invested ₹15,000 in a lump sum at the start, when the NAV was ₹100, you would have got only 150 units. Thus, with SISO, you ended up having a higher number of units.
2. At Exit (Systematic Out)
Just like the buying rate, SISO assists you in averaging the redemption costs, too. Instead of taking out the complete amount in one go, you receive a fixed income at regular intervals. This prevents
you from impulsively selling out all your funds. SISO helps manage market volatility and supports disciplined investing and withdrawals, which may contribute to long-term financial planning.
Why is RCA Important in Volatile Markets?
It is difficult to predict market fluctuations with the NAV rising or going down all of a sudden. A lump sum investment or withdrawal during such periods may lead to huge losses. By using a disciplined approach of investment over time, Rupee Cost Averaging lowers the risk and smoothens the returns.
Benefits of RCA with SISO
Here’s how implementing RCA with SISO can help to manage market volatility and supports disciplined investing and withdrawals:
● Reduced Impact of Volatility
With the systematic approach to making payments and withdrawals, SISO protects you from sudden market fluctuations.
● Disciplined Investing
Regular and systematic investments can help build a steady corpus over time without a considerable pocket pinch.
● Better Risk-Adjusted Returns
The market highs and lows get balanced out with RCA, and you may enjoy steady returns on your investments.
● Cash Flow Management
Systematic payouts ensure that you receive a fixed income at regular intervals, thereby managing your cash flow requirements.
● Emotional Advantage
SISO helps reduce the need for active market timing, offering a structured approach to investing and withdrawals.
For Example/Scenario
Suppose you contribute ₹10,000 in ULIP every month for 10 years. Total premium amount paid will be ₹12,00,000. As the contributions are spread out evenly, you benefit via RCA. When the market goes up, you get fewer units, and when the NAV goes down, you receive higher units. As time passes, this balances the overall purchase costs and may protect you from market swings.
After 10 years, you can withdraw the maturity benefit in the form of fixed monthly payouts under SISO. You can receive a fixed income every month, while the rest of the fund stays invested to yield additional returns.
SISO keeps you disciplined and helps in reducing the need for active market timing to enjoy steady investments and withdrawals.
Who Should Use SISO for RCA Benefits?
SISO is helpful in the following cases:
● For novice investors who are not experienced in handling market volatility. ● People who are planning for retirement, children’s education, or other major milestones of life can take advantage of SISO by disciplined investment and systematic withdrawals. ● Individuals looking for an alternative source of income can use SISO for a predictable monthly payout, while keeping the rest of the fund to grow.
Conclusion
Rupee Cost Averaging works well for investors who want steady growth without having to time the market. With Bajaj life insurance plans, you can choose SISO (only with select plans) and it helps in tackling market fluctuations through rupee-cost averaging. The disciplined approach to investment and redemption not only manages volatility but can also let you enjoy consistent returns to fulfil long-term goals.
